bull_thesis: MPWR is one of the few clean, profitable, net-cash ways to own the POWER-DELIVERY bottleneck of the AI build-out. bear_thesis: Everything good about MPWR is already in the price, and the price is the thesis-killer: ~112x trailing P/E and high-80s-to-90s forward P/E versus NXPI ~30x and ADI ~32x forward — a 3x premium to its own analog peer group, with all MoS methods reading deeply negative and a DCF fair value far below spot. Three cracks: (1) GROSS MARGIN IS FLAT at 55.5% for four straight quarters and management flagged 'strong headwinds potentially in the second half' of 2026 — premium AI mix is NOT translating into pricing power, the tell that the socket is contested, not owned. (2) COMPETITION is converging hard on the exact vertical-power/800V socket — TI launched a 'complete 800VDC architecture with NVIDIA' in March 2026, with Infineon, ADI, Renesas, Delta and Vicor all attacking; MPWR's defense ('we win on cost and lateral integration') is an incumbency-not-lock-in answer. consensus_delta: There is NO favorable consensus delta to arbitrage here — the street is looking the RIGHT way and is already euphoric ('Strong Buy', design-win narrative fully embraced). The genuine delta runs against the bulls: consensus implicitly assumes (a) the 55%+ gross margin will hold or expand on premium AI mix — but it has been FLAT at 55.5% for four straight quarters and management itself flagged H2-2026 'headwinds'; (b) the NVIDIA/vertical-power socket is durably MPWR's — but TI's March-2026 complete-800VDC-with-NVIDIA launch plus Infineon/ADI/Vicor convergence means socket share is contestable at every platform node; and (c) a ~90x forward multiple is sustainable through an AI-capex cycle. valley_of_death: No balance-sheet valley — $1.37B net cash, zero debt, strong FCF, no maturity wall. The near-term (3-9mo) risk is entirely valuation/sentiment and operational, not solvency: (1) the self-flagged 'strong headwinds potentially in the second half of 2026' could break the beat-and-raise cadence and de-rate a ~90x multiple hard; (2) inventory at 157 days is elevated for a +35% guide — any AI-server build-rate pause turns it into a digestion/write-down quarter; (3) the single-factor AI-capex dependence means a hyperscaler capex guide-down (a named regime tripwire — AI-capex is ~40% of 2026 S&P EPS growth) would de-gross the whole basket and hit MPWR's multiple disproportionately. The danger is a great print that simply isn't great ENOUGH for the multiple.
positioning_washout: Not a yield/passive-base liquidation story — the shareholder base is growth/momentum and index (S&P 500 member), with a thin 0.43% dividend. The washout risk is the inverse: a crowded, fully-extrapolated growth name where a single soft guide forces momentum/CTA and growth-mandate deleveraging in a name with a ~90x multiple and little valuation support beneath. One-directional insider selling into the re-rate (open-market sells dominating buys) signals those closest to the business are monetizing, not adding. forcing_function: The recurring forcing functions are the quarterly prints (next: Q2-2026 results ~late July 2026, guided $890-910M) and the H2-2026 gross-margin/'headwinds' tell management planted into the Q1 call. Further out, the NVIDIA 800V-DC / 1MW-rack production ramp (~2027) is the dated content catalyst. moderator_conclusion: PASS (C), conviction 2. MPWR is a genuinely excellent, accelerating, net-cash, founder-led franchise that sits squarely on a real AI-build-out bottleneck (vertical power delivery / 800V rack power) — the FORENSIC quality is high (credibility 4-5, STRENGTHENING, serial beat-and-raise, pristine balance sheet, best-in-class growth and returns in its peer group). # MPWR — Monolithic Power Systems — Forensic Dossier

*Interrogator: Claude Opus 4.8 · capital-allocation committee · transcripts Q1-2025 -> Q1-2026 (5 calls) cross-referenced against screener financials and live data as of 2026-06-21.*

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

The story across these five calls is a single, accelerating arc: "diversified analog grower" -> "the AI/data-center power-delivery winner that is still diversified."

- Q1-2025 (May): Cautious, hedged. Enterprise data framed as "flat plus or minus 20%," visibility "second half weighted." The thesis was design wins waiting to ramp — promise, not proof.
- Q2-2025 (Jul): Confidence inflects. "MPS has appeared to be a winner" in enterprise data; ASIC shipments begin; $4B SAM reaffirmed.
- Q3-2025 (Oct): "Layering of additional customers"; first enterprise-data growth guide of 30-40% for 2026. Hsing's candor — "if we didn't deliver 30-40%, the stock I see from $900 to $400" — signals he is watching the multiple.
- Q4-2025 (Feb): Floor raised to 50%; dividend +28% to $2; >72% of 3-yr FCF returned; $4B capacity milestone hit; clean CFO succession (Blegen->Dean).
- Q1-2026 (Apr): Floor raised to ~85% YoY enterprise-data growth; capacity target lifted $4B -> $6B; communications +33% sequential; DDR5 sampling.

Drivers kept: vertical power/modules, ASIC power, optical/co-packaged optics, 800V, 48V/zonal automotive, robotics/BMS. Drivers dropped: eMotion ("a flop"), data converters. Management is honest about misses — a credibility positive.

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)

Top line is genuinely re-accelerating (Rev FY24 2.21B -> FY25 2.79B; Q1-26 $804M +26% YoY; Q2 guide ~+35%). Enterprise Data +97.7% YoY to $262.8M validates the 85% floor. Gross margin honest but a yellow flag — pinned at the LOW end of the 55-60% model, 55.5% four straight quarters, not expanding despite mix shift. Returns high (ROE ~28%, ROIC ~18%) but ROIC ~18% on a ~113x P/FCF price is the whole problem. Net cash $1.37B, zero debt, >72% of 3-yr FCF returned, div +28%. Beat 7/7 quarters. The FY25 EPS '-65% YoY' line is a BASE-EFFECT ARTIFACT (FY24 inflated by a one-time gain); true TTM diluted EPS ~$13.87 (+41% latest-Q YoY) — the business did NOT shrink.

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY

RISING / offensive throughout. Q1-25 defensive-hedged -> Q3-25 combative ('$900 to $400') -> Q1-26 'raising the floor to ~85%.' Language is consistently growth/share-gain/capacity-build, never defensive-led. Only defensive notes: gross margin ('we're at the low end') and a single H2-2026 caution ('strong headwinds potentially in the second half'). Founder-CEO conviction backed by the numbers.

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY — HIGH

Escalating-beat pattern on the load-bearing ED line: 30-40% -> >=50% floor -> ~85% floor, then ED delivered +97.7% YoY. Sandbagging in the right direction; 7/7 beat rate. Risk is that the market has already extrapolated the beats.

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS

1. Customer concentration / single-GPU-platform share loss — the 2025 share-shift did happen, since replaced by ASIC/multi-customer layering (bad news cleared). 2. Gross margin stuck at the low end — the recurring non-answer; GM has NOT improved in four quarters. 3. Lumpy/short-lead-time backlog — partially resolved (book-to-bill >1, backlog into Q2-26). 4. Module-vs-chip margin dilution worry — management insists net-margin accretive.

## 6. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS

GREEN: ED +97.7% YoY validating the floor; net cash $1.37B / zero debt / +28% dividend / 72% FCF returned; clean CFO succession; candor on flops; capacity raised $4B->$6B demand-led. RED: gross margin stalled at 55.5% (bottom of model) four quarters with H2-2026 headwinds flagged; one-directional insider selling into the re-rate; valuation ~113x P/FCF, ~88x fwd P/E, NTM EV/EBITDA ~49x vs peer median ~21x — all MoS methods deeply negative; AI-capex single-factor dependence (same factor under the whole tape and the rest of Basket D).

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS

'Chip-only supplier' -> 'full-service silicon-based solution provider' in every prepared remark (ASP/mix-up narrative). 'Vertical power'/'module' frequency rises each quarter. Hsing's $900->$400 tell — he knows the stock is a sentiment vehicle on the ED line. New finance leadership more numeric/forthcoming. 'Headwinds in H2-2026' is the first forward caution planted into an otherwise euphoric call.

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT (for the director)

Classification: STRENGTHENING operationally — but the SECURITY is a valuation/expectations problem, not a business problem. The catalyst (AI/ED ramp) is already firing (+97.7% YoY) and fully extrapolated by a 'Strong Buy' street; there is no mispriced asymmetry to harvest. Moat: intangible/technology (power-density IP, BCD process, socket-level design-in) + scale-cost — high returns but margin flat-lined at the bottom of the model and H2 headwinds flagged, so NARROW-to-WIDE but STABLE, not widening. Secular force: MPWR is a BENEFICIARY, not a victim; no terminal-erosion theme applies; secular_threat = manageable (cyclical AI-capex digestion, not terminal demand loss). The single thing the director must weigh: a genuinely excellent net-cash compounder whose ENTIRE forward return is already priced at ~90x with the catalyst fired and insiders selling into it.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STRENGTHENING | MOAT: NARROW | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: manageable

*Interrogator: Claude Opus 4.8 · capital-allocation committee · transcripts Q1-2025 → Q1-2026 (5 calls) cross-referenced against screener financials and live data as of 2026-06-21.*

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

- **Q1-2025 (May):** Cautious, hedged. Enterprise data framed as "flat plus or minus 20%," visibility "second half weighted," management repeatedly refusing to forecast ("how good is good"). The thesis was *design wins waiting to ramp* — promise, not proof.
- **Q2-2025 (Jul):** Confidence inflects. "MPS has appeared to be a winner" in enterprise data; ASIC shipments begin; the $4B SAM reaffirmed. Still hedged on Q4 ("flat to down 20%") because of short lead times / thin backlog.

Michael Hsing's now-famous candor — *"if we didn't deliver 30–40%, the stock I see from $900 to $400, what kind of f***** is that?"* — signals he is watching the multiple and feels the pressure of expectations.

**Drivers introduced and kept:** vertical power / modules, ASIC power, optical/co-packaged optics, 800V data-center, 48V/zonal automotive, robotics/BMS. **Drivers quietly dropped:** eMotion ("kind of a flop," Hsing's own word, Q2-25); data converters ("slow moving… won't move the needle"). | Management claim | Financial evidence | Verdict |

**The one divergence that matters:** the FY25 EPS line in the scan reads "12.75, -65.2% YoY" — this is a **base-effect artifact**, not deterioration. FY24 NI of 1.79B / EPS 36.59 was inflated by a one-time item (a large non-operating/tax gain); normalized FY25 NI 616M and TTM diluted EPS 13.87 (+41% latest-Q YoY) are the true run-rate. ## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY

🔴 **Valuation: 113x P/FCF, ~114x P/E, NTM EV/EBITDA ~49x vs peer median ~21x.** All five MoS methods read -100%. Price $1,564 vs Buffett FV $1,208 (MoS -23%); DCF FV $238 (an outlier, but directionally screaming).
🔴 **AI-capex single-factor dependence** — the entire incremental thesis rides on hyperscaler/ASIC power demand, the same factor under the whole tape (regime doc: AI-capex ~40% of 2026 S&P EPS growth).

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS

- **Terminology shift:** "chip-only supplier" → "full-service silicon-based solution provider" is now in *every* prepared remark — a deliberate ASP/mix-up narrative.
- **"Vertical power" / "module" frequency** rises every quarter — the structural content-per-socket story.

**Classification: STRENGTHENING (operationally) — but the SECURITY is a valuation/expectations problem, not a business problem.**

**Reliability: HIGH.** This is a textbook sandbagger.

- **Q4-25 — clearly offensive:** non-ED markets +40%, "floor of 50%," Hsing interrupts the CFO to say "I thought we can do a lot more than that, conservatively." Confidence visibly re-rates up.
- **Q1-26 — peak offense, still backlog-anchored:** floor raised to 85%; capacity to $6B; Hsing's swagger ("I'll let Tony deliver better than our last CFO"; on OpEx leverage, dismisses the "big dollars = bigger gains" view as "bullshit"). Crucially the *quantitative* confidence (raised floors) is explicitly tied to **book-to-bill > 1 and backlog extending into Q2-26**, not vibes.

Shift from offensive→defensive language? **The opposite** — the file moves defensive→offensive over the window, which is the bullish direction. The only defensive residue is margin ("low end of our model," "strong headwinds potentially in the second half," Q1-26) and notebook/consumer caution.

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY

**Reliability: HIGH.** The forward-looking promises that have come due have been **met or beaten**:
- Enterprise-data 2026 guide *raised twice in two quarters* (50%→85%) and the actual Q1 print (+97.7%) is running ahead of even the raised floor — guidance has proven conservative in the direction that matters.
- Auto: management repeatedly said design wins would ramp "plus/minus a year"; 2023 and 1H-24 disappointed exactly as that framing warned, then auto delivered "two consecutive quarters of growth" (Q4-24) — the *framework* held even when timing slipped.
- Beat rate **7/7 (100%)**; Q1-26 beat on both lines ($804.2M vs ~$782M; EPS $5.10 vs $4.90). Q2-26 guided $890–910M.
- Demonstrable **sandbagging pattern**: Balow self-describes as "conservatively profiled," and the serial floor-raises are the signature of a team that guides low and clears it.

The one credibility caveat is **margin cadence**: management has promised "incremental sequential improvements of maybe 10–20 bps quarter over quarter" (Q4-25) for several quarters while GM has stayed pinned at ~55.2–55.8%. They have *not* delivered the margin-expansion cadence they keep teasing. Score the *revenue/EPS* guidance High; the *margin* guidance Medium.

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS

- **AI/non-AI and customer-concentration disclosure** is the recurring deflection. Analysts (Hans Mosesman Q4-24; Quinn Bolton Q3-24; Chris Caso Q1-26) repeatedly press for the AI vs non-AI split, the ASIC-vs-merchant split, and the size of the >10% customer. Management *consistently* refuses ("our customers have made it very clear," "we don't know how to separate it," "we're never putting a name out there"). There **is** at least one >10% direct customer (admitted Q3-24) — concentration risk is real and deliberately opaque.
- **Competition in enterprise data:** analysts pushed on dual-sourcing (Quinn Bolton Q3-24/Q4-24). Management conceded a "second or third competitor… will be introduced on the share" — then in 2025-26 simply stopped volunteering it while raising guidance. The risk did not disappear; it went unaddressed (see §6).
- **Margin:** Kelsey Chia (Q4-25) pinned them on the 55–60% target; the honest answer was "low side." Analysts who model margin expansion are leaning on a promise management keeps deferring.
- **2H-2026 caution:** in Q1-26 management volunteered "strong headwinds potentially in the second half" and stayed "cautious for the guidance in the second half" — a flag that the 85% ED floor and the full-year shape carry visible back-half risk (memory-price/notebook elasticity, auto timing, macro/geopolitical).

## 6. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS

🟢 **Re-accelerating top line** — 2025 +26.4% > 3-yr CAGR; Q1-26 +26% YoY with book-to-bill >1 and backlog extending forward. Growth is broadening (non-ED +40% in 2025), not narrowing to one socket.
🟢 **Fortress balance sheet** — $1.37B net cash, ROE 28.2%, ROIC 18.3%, Altman-Z 59.2; 13–14 straight years of growth. Capital return real (dividend +28%, $500M buyback).
🟢 **Guidance conservatism proven** — 7/7 beats; ED floor raised 50%→85% and still beaten.
🟢 **Underlying EPS growing** — TTM EPS $13.87, latest-Q EPS +41% YoY (the GAAP −65% is a 2024 one-time-benefit artifact, not deterioration).
🟢 **Founder-CEO with a 20-year share-gain playbook** in tight supply; preemptive inventory/capacity stance.

🔴 **Valuation is the thesis-killer** — 107.4x P/FCF, MoS −18% to fair value, all five intrinsic methods at −100% MoS. The stock embeds *years* of continued AI share gain; any timing slip de-rates a richly-priced multiple hard. (Peer comps: TXN ~20x FCF, ADI ~30x — MPWR is the richest analog semi in its group by 3–5x.)
🔴 **The competition caveat went silent, not away** — management openly warned (Q3-24/Q4-24) the AI socket would attract a "second/third/fourth" supplier, then dropped the topic while raising guidance. The enterprise-data hyper-growth is the single most crowded narrative in semis right now; a dual-source loss at the lead hyperscaler is the asymmetric downside.
🔴 **Customer concentration, deliberately opaque** — ≥1 customer >10% of revenue; management refuses granularity. AI capex is the dominant shared macro factor (per regime doc, ~40% of 2026 S&P EPS growth) — MPWR is highly correlated to it.
🔴 **Margin pinned at model floor** — GM stuck ~55.2–55.8% for 4–6 quarters; the "10–20 bps/qtr improvement" cadence keeps being promised and deferred; AI/data-center mix is GM-dilutive.

🔴 **Management volunteered 2H-26 caution** — "strong headwinds potentially in the second half."

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS

- **The disappearing dual-source warning** (detailed above) is the key terminology shift: in 2024 management *led* with "a second or third competitor will be introduced on the share"; by 2025-26 the phrase is gone from the enterprise-data discussion even as the dollars and guidance balloon. - **CFO transition** — Bernie Blegen (15 yrs, 40 earnings calls, 10 as CFO) retires; **Rob Dean** (9-yr controller) is interim CFO, with Tony Balow increasingly the financial voice. MPS frames this as a controlled controller→CFO succession ("ensures a lot of continuity"). Substantively low-risk, but it removes a long-trusted, candid voice (Blegen was the one who *gave* the dual-source caveat and the honest margin answers). - **Capacity-as-confidence signal** — raising the capacity target from $4B to $6B (Q1-26) is management putting capex behind the demand claim; it both corroborates the backlog story *and* explains the soft FCF conversion.

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT (for the director)

**Trajectory: STRENGTHENING.** Operationally this is one of the highest-quality compounders in the analog/mixed-signal space — re-accelerating revenue, fortress net-cash balance sheet, 7/7 beats, a proven founder, a guidance team that sandbags and clears, and a genuine secular tailwind (AI power-delivery + the modules/solutions ASP migration). The narrative arc is expansion, the tone trajectory is rising, and the load-bearing claim (ED +85% floor) is backlog-confirmed and already being beaten.

**The single most important thing the director must weigh before sizing:** *this is a fundamentally-strengthening business attached to a fully-saturated, premium price and a FIRED catalyst.* The AI-power re-rate is not ahead of you — it is behind you (Q1-26 already printed +97.7% ED growth, stock +6% on the print, targets lifted to $1,800–2,000). Size this as a high-quality momentum/quality holding to *trim or watch*, **not** as an asymmetric special-situation; under the current regime (independence-of-resolution-driver enforced, AI-capex flagged as the one factor that would de-gross everything), it is exactly the kind of single-shared-macro-factor name to underweight rather than press.

# MPWR — Monolithic Power Systems — Forensic Interrogator Dossier

*Interrogator: Claude Opus 4.8. Transcripts: Q2-2024 through Q1-2026 (7 consecutive calls). Financials: screener scan + FMP TTM as of 2026-03-31. This is a single-business analog/mixed-signal franchise pivoting from "chip-only" to "silicon-based solutions"; read it as a quality compounder, not a special situation.*

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

The core story is remarkably **consistent in structure and escalating in magnitude**. Across all seven calls Michael Hsing repeats the same three-part identity: (1) "we are transforming from a chip-only semiconductor supplier to a full-service silicon-based solutions provider" — this exact sentence is read verbatim in Q2-24, Q3-24, Q4-24, Q4-25 and paraphrased in Q1-26; (2) "diversity" is the moat — management refuses to be defined as an AI company ("we will not be known to be a AI power supply company," Q3-24; "we don't want to turn into an AI company only," Q2-24); (3) design wins ramp on a "plus/minus 12-18 month" clock that management explicitly says it cannot and will not time ("My accuracy is a plus minus 12 months," Q2-24; "I don't measure quarter by quarters, I measure by multiple years").

What **changed** is which engine is doing the lifting and how loudly management will quantify it:
- **Q2-24 → Q3-24:** AI/enterprise data is introduced as the breakout driver ("$500 million… 20% of total revenue," Q2-24) but immediately hedged — Q3-24 enterprise data went *down* ~1.5% sequentially and management pivoted hard to "diversity" and "we don't control our customers' ordering patterns." The narrative was defensive about the very segment the market cared most about.
- **Q4-24 → Q4-25:** The "second/third/fourth competitor will enter" warning on enterprise data (Q3-24, Q4-24) — a candid admission that the crown-jewel AI socket would be dual-sourced — quietly **recedes**. By Q4-25 the message flips from defense to offense: non-enterprise-data markets "grew by over 40% YoY," module revenue hit a record, and the enterprise-data guide is escalated from "flattish/even" (Q4-24) to a "floor of 50% growth" (Q4-25).
- **Q1-26 (the load-bearing call):** The floor is raised again to "around 85% year-over-year growth," with Tony Balow — the conservative new financial voice — explicitly framing it as backlog-confirmed, not a model change ("nothing has changed other than be able to see more orders in the books"). New multi-year drivers are seeded: robotics/humanoid ($150 content/Analyst Day), 800V silicon-carbide data-center power, co-packaged optics, DDR5 high-speed interface, building automation/audio. Capacity target raised from $4B to "$6B in the near future."

Net: the arc is **expansion, not drift**. Drivers are added, not dropped; the only thing quietly retired is the competitive-erosion caveat — which is the single most important narrative tell in the file (see §6, §7).

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)

The **one number that contradicts the glossy tape** is the headline EPS line in the scan: "EPS 12.75 latest | **-65.2% YoY**" and "2024 NI 1.79B, EPS 36.59 → 2025 NI 616M, EPS 12.75." # MPWR (Monolithic Power Systems) — Interrogator Forensic Dossier
**Sector:** Technology (Analog/Power Semiconductors) · **Signal type:** catalyst · **Window:** Q2 2024 → Q1 2026 (8 consecutive earnings calls, most recent 2026-04-30)

---

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

Across eight quarters MPS tells one remarkably consistent meta-story — *"we are transforming from a chip-only semiconductor supplier to a full-service silicon-based solutions provider"* — repeated verbatim in the prepared remarks of literally every call. That is the spine. What *moves* underneath it is the rotation of growth drivers and, above all, the maturation of the **enterprise-data / AI-power** narrative from a volatile question mark into the dominant, dated catalyst.

- **Q2–Q3 2024 (the AI surge):** Enterprise data is the obsession. Q2'24 revenue $507M is "yet another record … attributed to increased demand for AI power solutions." Hsing frames AI as ~20% of revenue ("a full $500 million") and immediately hedges: *"we don't want to turn into an AI company only"* (Q2'24). The narrative is offensive — design wins "waiting in the pipeline," power isolation as a "Greenfield opportunity in the hundreds of millions," vertical power, silicon carbide developed in-house since 2016.
- **Q3 2024 (the wobble):** Enterprise data goes *down ~1.5% sequentially* and management discloses the strategic crack candidly: *"that's going to require a second or third competitor to be introduced on the share"* (Blegen). This is MPS pre-announcing it will *lose share at its largest AI customer* because that customer wants supply-chain redundancy. The story pivots hard to **diversification** (auto +28% seq, comms +65% seq) to offset the AI air pocket.
- **Q4 2024 – Q1 2025 (the trough + diversification proof):** Management guides enterprise data *"flat … plus or minus 20%"*, even *"it will be down"* (Q4'24). The narrative deliberately de-emphasizes AI and showcases the non-AI engine: auto's third straight quarter of double-digit sequential growth, comms recovery, memory/notebook, robotics, modules. Tariff/supply-chain diversification ("half of capacity outside China") becomes a feature.
- **Q2–Q3 2025 (the inflection):** ASIC ramps begin — *"initial shipments of our power solutions to support our customers' new ASIC-based AI products"* (Q2'25). Hsing declares MPS *"appeared to be a winner"* in enterprise data. Guidance ratchets up: Q3'25 enterprise data +20-30% sequential; by Q3'25 call, 2026 enterprise data framed at **+30-40%**. Non-enterprise-data markets grow *over 40% YoY* — the diversification thesis is validated in the numbers.
- **Q4 2025 – Q1 2026 (the escalation):** The guidance escalator goes vertical. Q4'25: enterprise-data 2026 floor raised to **+50%**, Hsing scoffing *"I thought we can do a lot more than that."* Q1'26 (2026-04-30): floor raised again to **~+85% YoY**, with management explicitly stating the raise is *"not because there's been a fundamental change in the growth drivers … it's really … more comfort about what's in backlog"* — i.e., backlog now extends into Q2'26 with book-to-bill "well in excess of one." Capacity target lifted from $4B to a new **$6B** goal. CFO transition announced (Bernie Blegen retiring after 40 calls; Rob Dean interim).

**Drivers quietly dropped or downgraded:** e-commerce ("a kind of flop," Q2'25), data converters ("kind of slow moving … will not move the needle"), eMotion (admitted to grow *slower* than corporate average for years, only now reframed via robotics). ## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)

The single most important forensic fact in this file: **the screener's headline "EPS -65.2% YoY" and "NI 616M (2025) vs NI 1.79B (2024)" is an accounting artifact, not a business collapse.** 2024 net income of $1.79B on $2.21B revenue (an impossible ~81% net margin) was inflated by a large one-time tax benefit / deferred-tax item; 2025 net income of $616M on $2.79B revenue (~22% net margin) is the *normalized* figure. A director must not read the -65% EPS line as deterioration — every operating line says the opposite.

**Where the story is POORER than reality:** the headline EPS line. Anyone screening on "-65.2% EPS YoY" or "beat 7/7 but EPS collapsed" would wrongly flag deterioration. The business is demonstrably stronger, not weaker.

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY

**Rating: RISING — and measurably so, quantified by the enterprise-data guidance escalator.**

- **Q4'24–Q1'25 (cautious trough):** *"it will be down"* (enterprise data), *"flattish year … we don't know"* (Hsing). Hedged, conservative, leaning on diversification.

Tony Balow: the raise reflects *"more comfort about what's in backlog … extended ordering pattern"* — confidence now anchored to backlog rather than hope.

**Offensive vs defensive language:** decisively shifted from defensive (Q3'24–Q1'25: macro caution, share loss, "out of our control" ramp timing) to offensive (Q3'25–Q1'26: socket wins, capacity expansion, floor raises). The *only* defensive notes remaining are honest ones: notebook caution, mid-50s gross margin at low end, and a flagged *"strong headwinds potentially in the second half"* (Q1'26) — management is not pretending the macro is clean.

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY

**Reliability: HIGH (4/5).** This is the strongest pillar in the file.

- **Beat rate: 7/7 quarters (100%)** per the financials, corroborated by every call opening with "above the high end of guidance" / "record." MPS has not missed a quarter in this window.
- **The enterprise-data guidance arc is a *conservatism* showcase, not a credibility risk.** Track the same number across four consecutive calls: flat±20% → 30-40% → **50% floor** → **85% floor**. Management *raised guidance four times in a row*, each time explicitly citing realized backlog. Tony Balow repeatedly self-describes as wanting to *"stay pretty conservatively profiled"* and only raising as orders land. This is textbook **sandbagging** — the opposite of a company that over-promises. The risk is not that they miss; it is that the *stock* has already priced the upside revisions.
- **Multi-year delivery:** Hsing's recurring boast — *"for 21 years as a public company, we only have one year, 2010, we didn't grow … we missed \$3 million"* (Q2'25) — is consistent with the 14 consecutive growth years and is verifiable in the trajectory (rev 2021 $1.21B → 2025 $2.79B).

They are reliable on *direction and magnitude over a year*, deliberately unreliable on *quarter-by-quarter segment splits*. A director gets a credible annual trajectory, not a precise quarterly path.

**Score: High.** Beats are real, guidance revisions are upward and backlog-grounded, and the multi-year record is corroborated.

- **Single vs multi-source sockets:** Bolton (Q2'25) asks directly whether ASIC platforms are sole-sourced. Hsing's answer — *"a variety of single sourced and double sourced, multiple sourced … they all choose"* — is a non-answer that *confirms MPS does not have guaranteed sole-source lock-in* on the AI ramp. interrogator_dossier:
# Forensic Dossier — Monolithic Power Systems (MPWR)

*A deep dive on management and the setup, built from six consecutive quarterly earnings calls (Q1 2025 through Q1 2026) cross-referenced against the screener's hard financials.*

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

Monolithic Power Systems makes power-management chips — the small components that step electricity down to the precise voltages a processor needs. The story across these six calls is a clean, accelerating one: a diversified analog chipmaker that quietly became one of the prime power suppliers to the artificial-intelligence (AI) data-center build-out.

At the start of the window (Q1 2025, call dated 2025-05-01), management was deliberately cautious about its highest-profile business, enterprise data (the chips that power AI servers). They framed it as "flat plus or minus 20%" for the year and repeatedly stressed that visibility was "second-half weighted" and "outside of our control." The emphasis then was on diversification — automotive, storage, communications, consumer — and a long-running pitch to evolve "from being a chip-only semiconductor supplier to a full-service silicon-based solutions provider" (modules and systems, not just chips).

The arc from there is one of steadily rising confidence on the same core driver. The enterprise-data guidance walked up the ladder over four calls: flat-to-down-20% (Q2 2025) → "30% to 40%" growth for 2026 (Q3 2025, 2025-10-30) → "a floor of 50%" (Q4 2025, 2026-02-05) → "around 85% year-over-year growth" (Q1 2026, 2026-04-30). Alongside it, the capacity target was raised from a "$4 billion" plan to "$6 billion in the near future." New growth vectors were introduced and given weight as they matured: vertical power and modules for custom AI chips (ASICs), optical-module and switch power for communications (which "grew 33% sequentially" in Q1 2026), 48-volt and zonal automotive architectures, DDR5 memory interface products, and robotics/humanoid content. The "solutions provider" transformation thread is the one constant — it runs through every call as the long-term margin and content story.

Nothing material was quietly dropped. The data-converter business was openly described as "kind of slow moving" and not a needle-mover, and the eMotion/motor effort was candidly called "kind of a flop" early before robotics revived it — a notable honesty.

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)

| Management claim | The hard numbers | Verdict |
|---|---|---|
| "Fourteenth consecutive year of growth" (Q4 2025); only one down year in 21 years | Revenue 1.21B→1.79B→1.82B→2.21B→2.79B (2021–2025); 3-yr CAGR +15.9%, latest YoY +26.4% | Matches — durable, real top-line compounding |
| Enterprise-data accelerating to an "85% floor" | Independently confirmed: enterprise-data revenue grew 97.7% YoY in Q1 2026 to ~$263M (web-verified) | Over-delivered — the claim is conservative vs the print |
| Gross margins "in the range but on the low side" (55–60% model) | Gross margin 55.2%, called "stable" | Matches — honest framing; margins are flat, not expanding |
| Capital returned to holders: "over 72% of free cash flow" via buybacks/dividends; dividend +28% to $2/qtr | Net cash 1.37B (cash exceeds total debt of $0); TTM free cash flow $628M | Matches — genuinely net-cash, shareholder-friendly |
| The growth is "broad-based," not one-customer | Non-enterprise-data markets "grew by over 40%" in 2025; communications +33% sequentially Q1 2026 | Matches — diversification is real, not just rhetoric |

The one place the story is *richer than the numbers justify on a value basis* is valuation, not operations. Earnings per share (EPS) reads "-65.2% YoY" in the annual block — but that is an accounting artifact: 2024 net income of $1.79B included a large one-time item that inflated that year's EPS to $36.59; the cleaner trailing-twelve-month (TTM) diluted EPS is $13.87 and the latest quarter grew +41% YoY. Free cash flow is the soft spot — TTM free cash flow of $628M is well below the $2.79B revenue base, putting the stock at roughly 95x price-to-free-cash-flow. The operations are excellent; the price assumes they stay excellent for a very long time.

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY

**Trajectory: Rising.** Management grew progressively *more* specific and *more* confident on the load-bearing business across every call.

- Q1 2025: hedged — "we're not forecasting and how good is good"; "let us deliver numbers."
- Q2 2025: founder Michael Hsing's tell — "I'll give you an arrogant answer... I don't listen to any other costs" — supreme confidence in execution, dismissive of macro hand-wringing.
- Q3 2025: a striking, unscripted moment — Hsing on the 30–40% target: "If we didn't deliver 30% to 40%, the stocks I see from $900 to $400, what kind of [expletive] is that?" — raw conviction tied directly to the share price.
- Q4 2025 / Q1 2026: confidence migrated from the founder's gut to the order book — the new finance team grounded the 85% floor in "backlog... extending out into Q2 2026" and a "book-to-bill ratio well in excess of one." This is the healthiest kind of confidence shift: from narrative to signed orders.

The language is consistently offensive (growth, design wins, capacity expansion), never defensive. The lone note of caution is honest and recurring: short lead times mean limited visibility beyond two quarters, and they flagged "strong headwinds potentially in the second half" on notebooks/consumer.

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY

**Reliability: High.** The screener records a beat in 7 of the last 7 quarters (100%). More tellingly, the *forward* guidance proved serially conservative: every enterprise-data number management gave was later raised, and the final 85% floor was itself beaten by the ~98% actual print. CFO Bernie Blegen explicitly described his style — "I like to stay pretty conservatively profiled when I make an estimate" — and the record bears it out. This is a sandbagging pattern in the investor-friendly direction: guide low, raise often, deliver above. The capacity walk-up ($4B → $6B) is the same pattern applied to the supply side.

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS

Analysts pressed hardest on three themes, and management's answers improved over time rather than curdling into bad news:
- **Customer concentration / share loss.** Early in 2025 there was real worry about a platform change at the largest AI customer hurting MPWR's share. Management deflected specifics ("we don't comment on specific customers") but the diversification into multiple ASIC customers ultimately defused it — by Q2 2025 they guided back toward "no customer more than mid-high single digits."
- **Gross margin.** Analysts repeatedly probed why margins sit at the low end of the 55–60% model and when they expand. Management's answer stayed honest and unchanged: expansion needs longer backlog visibility; expect only "10 to 20 basis points quarter over quarter" improvement. No margin miracle is promised — a green flag for credibility, a yellow flag for the multiple.
- **Lead-time / visibility.** The recurring caveat that order patterns are short and backlog thin was raised every quarter; by Q4 2025 it had flipped to a positive (backlog finally building) — the worry resolved in shareholders' favor.

## 6. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS

🟢 Enterprise-data guidance raised four straight times and still beaten — a rare credibility signature.
🟢 Genuine net-cash balance sheet ($1.37B cash, no debt); ~72% of free cash flow returned to holders; dividend +28%.
🟢 Diversification is real — non-AI markets grew 40%+ in 2025, so the story does not hinge on a single socket.
🟢 Management candor — openly called eMotion "a flop" and data converters slow; founder ties his own credibility to the share price.
🟢 Smooth, telegraphed CFO succession (Blegen → Dean), an internal promotion continuing a long tradition.

🔴 Valuation, not operations, is the risk: ~95x price-to-free-cash-flow and a roughly -8% margin of safety (the stock trades *above* the screener's fair value). Every internal valuation model (discounted-cash-flow, earnings-power, Graham, owner-earnings) flags -100% margin of safety.
🔴 Insider behavior, open-market only: zero buys against 93 sells (~188k shares). No insider is buying at this price.
🔴 Gross margin is flat at the low end of the model — the "solutions provider" mix shift has not yet lifted reported margins.
